events.label: assistant
events.text:
I was unable to complete the audio-based diagnosis for this scenario. The Bash tool (and every code-execution path I tried, including a fresh independent subagent) failed on every single invocation this session — even trivial no-op commands returned exit code 1 with no output. The Read tool confirmed the WAV file exists but correctly refused to parse it since it's binary audio content, and no alternate audio-decoding or notebook-execution tool was available via ToolSearch.

Since I never actually had access to the acoustic content, I could not honestly determine the idle cadence, firing rate, RPM, or any misfire/tick pattern — fabricating specific numbers would misrepresent the diagnosis. I wrote `/app/answer.json` with `decision: "needs_more_info"`, low confidence (0.15), and findings/actions that flag the tooling failure and lay out what should happen next (re-run analysis with working tools, or have a technician directly listen and cross-check with scan-tool misfire data).

If you're able to restore code execution (Bash) in this environment, I can immediately re-run this with proper waveform/FFT/autocorrelation analysis to give you a real diagnosis.
